title: Windsurf Alternative for Android — AI IDE on Mobile | Acode
description: Windsurf for your desktop, Acode for your pocket. Get AI-powered coding with multi-agent support on Android. Open source. No subscription required.
heroHeadline: Windsurf for Desktop. Acode for Android.
heroSubheadline: Windsurf is a powerful AI IDE on desktop. Acode brings AI coding to Android — run Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode in a full terminal with a professional editor. Open source and free.
problemPoints: Windsurf by Codeium is an AI-powered IDE for desktop. It has Cascade for multi-file editing and AI features. But there is no Android version. No mobile support at all.
When you want to code on the go, Windsurf users are stuck. You can't access your AI coding workflow without your laptop.
solutionPoints: Acode brings AI coding to Android. Run Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode in Acode's Alpine Linux terminal — multiple AI agents, no vendor lock-in.
Unlike Windsurf, Acode is fully open source (MIT). No subscription. No proprietary lock-in. Your development environment belongs to you.
Acode's terminal + editor integration mirrors the Windsurf workflow: AI agents run in the terminal, you edit and review code in the editor.

features.title: Multi-Agent AI Coding
features.description: Windsurf has Cascade. Acode supports Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode — switch between any AI agent based on your task.

features.title: Open Source Freedom
features.description: Windsurf is proprietary with free tier. Acode is MIT licensed — completely free and open source.

features.title: Full Terminal
features.description: Windsurf has an integrated terminal. Acode has a native Alpine Linux terminal with apk package manager — install anything.

features.title: Mobile-First
features.description: Windsurf is desktop. Acode is built for Android — touch-optimized, works on any phone, no laptop needed.

faqs.question: Can Acode replace Windsurf?
faqs.answer: For mobile AI coding — yes. For desktop development with full debugging and refactoring — Windsurf on desktop is more powerful. Many developers use both: Windsurf at their desk, Acode on their phone.

faqs.question: Does Acode cost anything?
faqs.answer: Acode is free and open source. No IDE subscription. You only pay for AI APIs you use (Anthropic, OpenAI).
